attestation_items.description: Stage198 must preregister the reduced-scope claim boundary before credit attestation.
Manual attestation must include an explicit reduced-scope cost estimate.
User attestation does not change the preregistered reduced-precision interpretation boundary.

evidence.lower_precision_caveat: A win at 2048 shots can support a reduced-precision hardware positive, but it cannot be described as the original 4096-shot evidentiary run. Any marginal result below 2 shot quanta is inconclusive, not a loss or win.

attestation_items.description: This gate can record whether the user has verified IBM credit/billing/Runtime allowance, but it never approves live execution.
Exact approval phrase and live-runner preparation remain later gates even after credit attestation.

excluded: creation of a runnable live-submit command
a noisy-hardware robustness or auditability conclusion
supported: explicit reduced-scope credit attestation prompt with cost estimate
reduced-scope exposure and interpretation boundary are attached before asking the user
credit attestation remains separate from exact approval and live-runner preparation

disallowed_interpretations: claiming equivalence to the full 4096-shot Stage190 package
dropping any comparator family after hardware results are known
using the 512-shot scout boundary for robustness claims
treating directional seed577-only evidence as independent-seed replication
changing thresholds after hardware counts are collected

pass_fail_policy.lower_precision_caveat: A win at 2048 shots can support a reduced-precision hardware positive, but it cannot be described as the original 4096-shot evidentiary run. Any marginal result below 2 shot quanta is inconclusive, not a loss or win.

interpretation_boundary.selected_lanes: all four Stage189/190 IBM replacement lanes

manual_attestation_prompt: Please verify in IBM that the selected instance has enough remaining credit/billing/Runtime allowance for the reduced-scope run `all_lanes_half_shots_2048`: 324 row-level jobs, 659360 total shots, 2048 shots per packet row, and a $25.00 local cap. Using IBM's documented $1600/1000-quantum-second example, this cap allows 15.625 quantum seconds, so this reduced run fits only if effective quantum time is <= 23.70 microseconds/shot. If you attest yes, you are attesting account-side allowance and willingness to spend within this cap only; this still does not approve live submission or accept the exact approval phrase.
next_gate: Present this reduced-scope estimate to the user. If the user attests IBM credit allowance and willingness to spend within the cap, rerun Stage199 with human_credit_allowance_verified=true, then run exact approval review.
objective: Determine whether PhaseWrap-RoPE's compact phase-wrap positional score has measurable robustness or auditability advantages on noisy quantum hardware, compared with matched positional-score encodings, under fixed circuit width.
